First, I know that duodenal ulcers are usually caused by Helicobacter pylori, but sometimes they can be due to Zollinger-Ellison syndrome.
Zollinger-Ellison is a condition where the stomach produces too much acid, which can lead to ulcers.
The high basal acid secretory outputs suggest that her stomach is producing a lot of acid, which is a key feature of Zollinger-Ellison.
The secretin stimulation test is used to diagnose Zollinger-Ellison.

Her levels being over 1000 pg/mL are definitely elevated, which points towards this condition.
She also has a history of ulcers that haven't responded to treatment, which makes sense because Zollinger-Ellison can be resistant to standard therapies.
So, putting it all together, the most likely diagnosis is Zollinger-Ellison syndrome.
